message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "quote for 3 runes block (newcomer) returns 71"
Current error: Expected 71, received 88 (block rule not yet applied)
Passing tests so far: 7

Per MHPCO rules: A "building block" of 3 alike components is offered at a special base premium of 60 G (instead of 3×25 = 75). For 3 runes: base = 60. Newcomer premium = 60 + 6 + 5 = 71.

Important detail: block requires EXACTLY 3 alike components. 4 runes is NOT a block (it stays 100). 6 runes would be? — but worry about that later if a test demands it. Be minimal.

For this test: detect when items consist of exactly 3 alike components and substitute base premium 60.

Keep all 7 previous tests green.

Run `pnpm test`.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll start by reading the current state of the test and implementation files.
Now I'll implement the minimal change to detect a block of exactly 3 alike components.

input.old_string:
const basePremiumFor = (items: { type: string }[]): number =>
  items.reduce((sum, item) => sum + BASE_PREMIUMS[item.type], 0);

content.text:
All 8 tests pass (7 previous + the new "3 runes block" test).

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added a guard in `basePremiumFor` that returns 60 when items consist of exactly 3 alike components. Falls through to the existing sum logic otherwise.
**Result**: All tests now pass (8 passing, 20 todo).
**Approach**: Minimal conditional check using `items.length === 3` and `every` against `items[0].type`. Hardcoded base value of 60 G as the spec dictates. No generalization to other block sizes or multi-block scenarios — those will come when their tests demand them.

Relevant files:
